---
title: "Governance, Ethics & Equity Track"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
Self-paced track · 7 topics · novice → moderate
:::

[← All self-paced tracks](../tracks.qmd){.track-back}

The judgment layer: how to verify AI output, protect data, weigh environmental cost, and keep AI use fair and accountable in public-health work. These pages deepen the ethics thread that runs through every workshop. Start from **[Workshop 1](../workshop1.qmd)**.

## Difficulty levels

[Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} &nbsp; getting started — no prior experience needed.
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} &nbsp; comfortable with some AI use.
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} &nbsp; experienced users going deep.

## Topics

| # | Topic | Level | You'll be able to… |
|---|-------|-------|--------------------|
| 1 | [Verifying AI Output](verifying-output.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | A repeatable workflow to catch hallucinations and bad citations |
| 2 | [AI & Environmental Sustainability](environmental-sustainability.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | The energy and water cost of AI, and how to right-size use |
| 3 | [Data Security & Classification Deep-Dive](data-security-classification.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Match every tool to Drexel's data classification |
| 4 | [Responsible AI](responsible-ai.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Accountability, transparency, and governance in practice |
| 5 | [AI Bias & Health Equity Audit](bias-equity-audit.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Test AI outputs across populations, not just on average |
| 6 | [Keeping Your Judgment: Cognitive Offloading](judgment-and-offloading.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Use AI to amplify judgment, not replace it; know where to scrutinize |
| 7 | [AI in Mentoring & Advising](mentoring-advising.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Where AI helps with advising tasks and where the relationship is paramount |

## Not sure where to start?

Answer a few questions to find your starting point in this track.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions":[{"q":"A colleague wants to paste interview transcripts into ChatGPT for thematic coding. What's the first question to ask?","options":["What is the data classification — could any participant be identified?","Which AI model gives the best themes?","How long are the transcripts?","Has the IRB approved the study?"],"answer":0,"explain":"If data classification is your first instinct, you can skip to topic 4 (Responsible AI) or topic 5 (Bias Audit). If you're unsure about data rules, start at topic 3 (Data Security & Classification)."},{"q":"You notice an AI health-screening tool performs worse for Black patients than white patients. What best describes this problem?","options":["Algorithmic bias that could worsen health disparities","A minor accuracy issue that will improve with more data","A user error in how prompts were written","An expected trade-off in any predictive model"],"answer":0,"explain":"If you can identify algorithmic bias and its equity implications, start at topic 5 (Bias & Equity Audit) or topic 6 (Cognitive Offloading). If this framing is new, begin at topic 1 (Verifying AI Output)."},{"q":"What's the risk of routinely accepting AI-generated answers without critical evaluation?","options":["You gradually lose the ability to catch errors and exercise independent judgment","The AI learns your preferences and gets too personalized","You use up your AI quota faster","Others will think you're not doing your own work"],"answer":0,"explain":"If cognitive offloading is a concept you already consider, start at topic 6 (Cognitive Offloading) or topic 7 (Mentoring). If this is a new idea, begin at topic 1 (Verifying AI Output)."}]}
</script>
</div>

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules apply throughout
Use **public or non-sensitive** data for every activity, and match any tool to Drexel's data classification. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
